nodes.name: Arthur River (Tasmania)
nodes.summary: Arthur River (Tasmania)是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联1种鸟类，包括橙腹鸚鵡，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 海岸湿地
nodes.summary: 海湾、泻湖、盐沼与河口组成的复合湿地。

nodes.name: Port MacDonnell, South Australia
nodes.summary: Port MacDonnell, South Australia是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联1种鸟类，包括橙腹鸚鵡，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 橙腹鸚鵡
nodes.summary: 橙腹鸚鵡（Orange-bellied parrot，学名 Neophema chrysogaster），在图谱中标记为极危（CR）物种，已关联4个分布地点节点，常见栖息地包括海岸湿地、河口、沼泽，主要威胁包括栖息地破坏、外来捕食者。
nodes.shortSummary: 橙腹鸚鵡（学名：Neophema chrysogaster），又名黃腹長尾鸚鵡，是澳洲特有的一種鸚鵡。雄鳥的上身呈鮮草綠色，下身黃色，腹部橙色。雌鳥及雛鳥的綠色較沉。牠們前額都有藍色斑紋，外飛羽藍色。牠們主要吃種子及草莓。 橙腹鸚鵡分佈在塔斯曼尼亞，會到澳洲大陸南部的沿海草原過…

nodes.name: 外来捕食者
nodes.summary: 岛屿鸟类常受猫、鼬和鼠等外来种影响。

nodes.name: 鸟纲
nodes.summary: 鸟纲（Aves）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联10767种鸟类，如丹顶鹤、朱鹮、中华秋沙鸭等10767种鸟类。

nodes.name: 栖息地破坏
nodes.summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。

nodes.name: 河口
nodes.summary: 淡水与海水交汇形成的高生产力边缘生态系统。

nodes.name: 沼泽
nodes.summary: 具有较高含水量和密生植被的浅水环境。

nodes.name: CR
nodes.summary: CR表示 IUCN 极危等级，当前关联174种鸟类，如勺嘴鹬、加州神鹫、菲律宾鹰等174种鸟类。

nodes.name: 脊索动物门
nodes.summary: 脊索动物门（Chordata）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联10767种鸟类，如丹顶鹤、朱鹮、中华秋沙鸭等10767种鸟类。

nodes.name: 塔斯馬尼亞州
nodes.summary: 塔斯馬尼亞州是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联47种鸟类，包括澳大利亚麻鸭、Brown quail、Hoary-headed grebe等47种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 动物界
nodes.summary: 动物界（Animalia）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联10767种鸟类，如丹顶鹤、朱鹮、中华秋沙鸭等10767种鸟类。

nodes.name: Neophema
nodes.summary: Neophema（Neophema）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联6种鸟类，如藍翅鸚鵡、Elegant parrot、Rock parrot等6种鸟类。

nodes.name: Canunda National Park
nodes.summary: Canunda National Park是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联1种鸟类，包括橙腹鸚鵡，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The orange-bellied parrot breeds in Tasmania and winters on the coast of southern mainland Australia, foraging on saltmarsh species, beach or dune plants and a variety of exotic weed species.Background and implementation information for the Orange-bellied Parrot Recovery Plan, The Orange-bellied Parrot Recovery Team (2006), Department of Primary Industries and Water (DPIW), Hobart, p

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: In late 2017, there was a possible sighting at Canunda National Park in South Australia; the most recent confirmed sighting in the state being five years beforehand at Port MacDonnell. In July 2021, two individuals were recorded at Arthur River in northwestern Tasmania; it is unclear whether they had returned early from the mainland or did not migrate across the Bass Strait at all. They are commonly found in southern Australia

links.label: 栖息于
links.evidence: On the way, they may stop (and occasionally overwinter on) King Island, particularly Lake Flannigan. The few mainland sites are estuaries and lagoons that contain their favoured salt marsh habitat and are generally within 3 km of the coast. These include sites in or close to Port Phillip such as Werribee Sewage Farm, the Spit Nature Conservation Reserve, the shores of Swan Bay, Swan Island, Lake Connewarre State Wildlife Reserve, Lake Victoria and Mud Islands, as well as French Island in Western Port

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: The 2000 Action Plan for Australian Birds identifies the following potential threats to the orange-bellied parrot: * Fragmentation and degradation of over-wintering habitat * Competition with introduced seed-eaters * Abandonment of former breeding habitat due to altered fire regime and competition for hollows (with the introduced common starling) * Random events due to the small size of the population * Disorientation from brightly lit fishing boats (during the migrations across Bass Strait) * Introduced predators * Disease (such as psittacine circoviral disease) Other identified potential threats include: * Lack of safety in numbers for a small bird attractive to avian predators (Brouwer and Garnett 1990) * Historically was trapped for aviculture (Garnett 1992) * A stomach virus is threatening a breeding program for the critically endangered orange-bellied parrot. In 2013, the Orange-bellied Parrot Recovery Team's Annual Report for 2012/13 (published October 2013) outlined the reasons for its decline and the threats to the orange-bellied parrot as follows: ...Current knowledge suggests that habitat loss and degradation, particularly in the non-breeding range, has caused the decline

In 2013, the Orange-bellied Parrot Recovery Team's Annual Report for 2012/13 (published October 2013) outlined the reasons for its decline and the threats to the orange-bellied parrot as follows: ...Current knowledge suggests that habitat loss and degradation, particularly in the non-breeding range, has caused the decline
